page: Microsoft Azure D4ns_v6 is a general-purpose, network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V as its hypervisor. The CPU features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the D4ns_v6 demonstrates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1695.38 and a PassMark CPU score of 7079.45. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance present notable tradeoffs. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 3448.02 and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression of 23.02 GB/sec are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth also falls into the lower tier, with uncached large-block read and write speeds measuring 32.41 GB/sec and 23.58 GB/sec respectively. For lightweight AI workloads, the instance achieves an average prompt processing speed of 580.96 tokens/second on a small 135M LLM model, though performance drops significantly to 29.96 tokens/second on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4ns_v6 offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring consistent CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage, as it features no local disk space and relies entirely on Premium Storage capabilities. This makes it a viable option for network-oriented tasks, static web serving (which reached 7.42 GB/sec in testing), and basic single-threaded processing. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases or highly parallelized multi-core computing due to its constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core benchmark limitations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 260 MB L3 cache, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal average single-core CPU performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 7079.45 and static web serving throughput of 7.42 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, as shown by a multi-core stress-ng score of 3448.02 and large-block read speeds of 32.41 GB/sec. This virtual machine is best suited for network-optimized workloads, static web serving, and lightweight single-threaded applications, while being less optimal for memory-bound databases or heavy multi-threaded compute tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and average single-core performance, suitable for static web serving and light workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ns_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and average single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-vCPU virtual machine offering balanced memory density and network optimization for general-purpose workloads without local storage.

Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No local storage, relying entirely on Premium Storage capabilities
Average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput
Tradeoffs include weak multi-core scaling and lower memory bandwidth
Best suited for network-optimized tasks, static web serving, and light single-threaded applications
